title.zh: Django：修复密码重置 token 与邮箱状态的关系
title.en: Django: fix how password reset tokens relate to email state

summary.zh: 审查一个 Django auth PR：作者称 password reset token 在邮箱状态异常时仍可能通过校验，并补了一条空邮箱用例。
summary.en: Review a Django auth PR: the author says password reset tokens can still validate around unusual email state, and adds an empty-email regression case.

reviewTarget.note: Adapted from a real upstream issue for review training. Whether this change should be merged is the question — reach your own conclusion before opening the source links.

pr.title: Avoid password reset token crashes for users without a usable email
pr.author: codegen-agent
body: PasswordResetTokenGenerator currently builds the token hash from stable user fields but assumes every user object has a meaningful email value during reset flows. Custom user models and imported accounts can have an empty email, and that edge case makes the reset path fragile.

This PR guards the hash construction with get_email_field_name(): when the configured email field is empty, the generator falls back to the existing password/login/timestamp material instead of depending on an empty email value.
Added a regression test for a user with an empty email to confirm token creation and validation still work. Existing auth tests pass.

background: Django password reset tokens are generated and checked by PasswordResetTokenGenerator. The token hash is derived from selected user state so old tokens stop working after important state changes.
Projects may customize the user model and the email field name. The review context includes the pre-patch token generator excerpt in src-tokens.py.
When reviewing token logic, distinguish between avoiding a narrow crash and preserving the invalidation contract for previously issued tokens.

notes: The patch does not bind the email value to the token hash. It only checks whether the current email field is empty, then returns exactly the same hash material as before. For normal users with a non-empty email, behavior is unchanged.

The security-relevant sequence is: issue a password reset token, change the user's email, then check the old token. This PR still accepts the old token because the changed email value is not part of _make_hash_value.
The new test proves only that an empty-email user can still create and validate a token. It does not reproduce the stale-token invalidation case and would pass even though the original bug remains.

learningGoal: 判断 token 哈希材料是否覆盖应使 token 失效的用户状态，而不是只看单个异常路径是否通过。

terms.description: Django 用来生成和校验密码重置 token 的组件。
参与 token 签名或哈希的用户状态。
相关状态变化后，旧 token 必须不再通过校验。
Django 自定义用户模型中用于定位邮箱字段的方法。

behaviorChecks.input: 生成 reset token 后更改用户邮箱
behaviorChecks.reviewQuestion: 补丁是否让 email 的实际值进入 token hash？

behaviorChecks.input: 用户使用自定义 email 字段名
behaviorChecks.expected: 修复应通过 get_email_field_name 读取当前邮箱值。
behaviorChecks.reviewQuestion: 补丁是否同时兼容字段名和字段值变化？

behaviorChecks.expected: 不应崩溃，但这个路径不能替代邮箱变更失效测试。
behaviorChecks.reviewQuestion: 新增测试证明的是 crash 防护还是 token invalidation？
